channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: Study finds educators speak differently to toddlers depending on gender
item.description: New research from the University of Queensland has found that early childhood educators unconsciously adjust the language they use with toddlers depending on the gender composition of the group, raising questions about how everyday interactions may influence children's social, emotional and cognitive development.
item.content:encoded: New research from the University of Queensland has found that early childhood educators unconsciously adjust the language they use with toddlers depending on the gender composition of the group, raising questions about how everyday interactions may influence children's social, emotional and cognitive development.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: MindChamps returns to profitability as Australia remains key growth market
item.description: MindChamps PreSchool has reported a return to profitability in FY2025, with the Singapore-listed early learning provider highlighting improved financial performance, expansion activity in Australia and plans for further franchise growth as it positions itself for future international expansion.
item.content:encoded: MindChamps PreSchool has reported a return to profitability in FY2025, with the Singapore-listed early learning provider highlighting improved financial performance, expansion activity in Australia and plans for further franchise growth as it positions itself for future international expansion.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Annual Wage Review 2026 decision to be live streamed on 2 June
item.description: The Fair Work Commission will hand down its Annual Wage Review 2026 decision at 10am AEST on Tuesday, 2 June 2026, with the announcement to be live streamed nationally.
item.content:encoded: The Fair Work Commission will hand down its Annual Wage Review 2026 decision at 10am AEST on Tuesday, 2 June 2026, with the announcement to be live streamed nationally.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: C&K Annual Report 2025 highlights workforce investment, quality gains and reform focus
item.description: Queensland Childcare Services (QCCS) is using National Reconciliation Week 2026 to go all in on its commitment to embedding reconciliation into everyday early childhood education.
item.content:encoded: Queensland Childcare Services (QCCS) is using National Reconciliation Week 2026 to go all in on its commitment to embedding reconciliation into everyday early childhood education.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Noongar culture and arts grow wellbeing literacy in early childhood education
item.description: New research has found that Noongar culture and arts can play a powerful role in strengthening young children’s capabilities to experience and communicate about their wellbeing.
item.content:encoded: New research has found that Noongar culture and arts can play a powerful role in strengthening young children’s capabilities to experience and communicate about their wellbeing.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Childcare leader ‘All In’ on reconciliation through early childhood education
item.description: Queensland Childcare Services (QCCS) is using National Reconciliation Week 2026 to go all in on its commitment to embedding reconciliation into everyday early childhood education.
item.content:encoded: Queensland Childcare Services (QCCS) is using National Reconciliation Week 2026 to go all in on its commitment to embedding reconciliation into everyday early childhood education.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Safety fears as foreign students use childcare courses as visa ‘back door’ - Daily Telegraph Sydney

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 29 May 2026
item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to NT child protection reforms, National Sorry Day, AbSec’s call for systemic change, the launch of the Bushland Animal Yoga Cards, and continued Budget coverage.
item.content:encoded: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to NT child protection reforms, National Sorry Day, AbSec’s call for systemic change, the launch of the Bushland Animal Yoga Cards, and continued Budget coverage.

item.title: From Country to Classroom: SNAICC Launches Culturally Grounded Learning Tools
item.description: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children has launched a unique culturally informed early Childhood Education and Care resource for early childhood workers. The Bushland Animal Yoga Cards’ offer ACCO and non-Indigenous ECEC services a resource that incorporates movement with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander languages, with native Australian animals representing various poses and movements.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children has launched a unique culturally informed early Childhood Education and Care resource for early childhood workers. The Bushland Animal Yoga Cards’ offer ACCO and non-Indigenous ECEC services a resource that incorporates movement with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander languages, with native Australian animals representing various poses and movements.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby Donations Update

item.title: To improve children’s mental health, start by supporting their parents
item.description: Long-term data shows children are more likely to develop mental health issues when their parents experience financial stress and housing instability.
item.content:encoded: Long-term data shows children are more likely to develop mental health issues when their parents experience financial stress and housing instability.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Leadership Calls for Real Action on Child Safety
item.description: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander leaders call for real action on child safety; warn NT law changes undermine Child Placement Principle.
item.content:encoded: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander leaders call for real action on child safety; warn NT law changes undermine Child Placement Principle.

item.title: SNAICC Raises Alarm Over Narrow NT Child Protection Inquiry Scope
item.description: SNAICC says narrow scope of NT Child Protection Inquiry fail to address systemic issues needed to keep Aboriginal children and families safe.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC says narrow scope of NT Child Protection Inquiry fail to address systemic issues needed to keep Aboriginal children and families safe.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Is baby talk bad? Why ‘parentese’ actually helps babies learn language
item.description: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.content:encoded: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 15 May 2026
item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, the Federal Budget was a missed opportunity for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities. NT Government has introduced legislation that undermine the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Child Placement Principle. And a heartwarming Mother's Day story from a member of SNAICC's Youth Voice advisory group on how love, kinship and culture can help children in out-of-home-care grow up Deadly.
item.content:encoded: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, the Federal Budget was a missed opportunity for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities. NT Government has introduced legislation that undermine the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Child Placement Principle. And a heartwarming Mother's Day story from a member of SNAICC's Youth Voice advisory group on how love, kinship and culture can help children in out-of-home-care grow up Deadly.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Sector Peak Urges NT Government to Scrap Legislative Changes and Partner on Child Protection
item.description: SNAICC is urging the NT Government to abandon proposed child protection legislative changes that would weaken the Child Placement Principle.

item.dc:creator: SNAICC
